extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is an inductive invariant preserved by every transition. The only transition that sets sent:=true is t_send_allowed, whose guard is `not opted_out` and which does not modify opted_out; thus its post-state has opted_out=false, satisfying the implication vacuously. The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is t_optout, which simultaneously sets sent:=false, so its post-state has sent=false. t_send_blocked sets sent:=false (so holds regardless of opted_out), and t_tick sets sent:=false (holds regardless). The initial state has opted_out=false (vacuous). Critically, while opted_out=true, a send_attempt matches t_send_blocked (guard opted_out), never t_send_allowed, so sent cannot be raised to true while opted_out is true. Therefore no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, and the property holds for the entire input space (the 10-event bound is irrelevant since the invariant is inductive).
